description: Baseline HA sizing catalog for MongoDB, Redis, and Runtime tiers. Used by sizing-live-xlsx.py to compute always-on baseline costs and enable tier-selection dropdowns.

dev-compressed.description: Current dev/QA shape: 500m request, 2 CPU limit, 1-2Gi memory. Matches measurement 7414138.
dev-compressed.notes: Measured: 13 msg/s per pod (25 VU), 26 msg/s fleet (2 pods). CFS throttle at 31% baseline.

standard.description: M-tier runtime: 2 CPU request, 4 CPU limit, 4-8Gi memory. ~2× dev-compressed throughput expected.
standard.notes: Extrapolated from S-tier: 2× CPU limit ≈ 2× throughput. Needs validation via M-tier saturation run.

performance.description: L-tier runtime: 4 CPU request, 8 CPU limit, 8-16Gi memory. High-throughput workloads.
performance.notes: Extrapolated from S-tier: 4× CPU limit ≈ 4× throughput. Needs validation via L-tier saturation run.

small.description: 3-replica set, 4c/16Gi per member, 256Gi PVC. Matches current dev infra-snapshot.
small.notes: At 26 msg/s measured only 1% CPU utilization (155m/16000m). Ceiling set conservatively at 50 msg/s with current IOPS.

medium.description: 3-replica set, 8c/32Gi per member, 512Gi PVC, 2300 IOPS. For 50-200 msg/s workloads.
medium.notes: Doubles CPU allocation and IOPS. Working set fits in WiredTiger cache. Supports 16 reads + 4 writes per msg.

large.description: 3-replica set, 16c/64Gi per member, 1024Gi PVC, 5000 IOPS. For 200-500 msg/s workloads.
large.notes: Full E32s_v5 equivalent. For high-throughput production with 4KB writes × 4 per message.

small.description: 1 master (2c/4Gi) + 3 replicas (1c/2Gi). Matches current dev infra-snapshot.
small.notes: At 26 msg/s measured only 1% CPU (33m). 145 Redis ops/msg = 3770 ops/s. Single-thread ceiling ~100K ops/s. Conservative ceiling 100 msg/s (14.5K ops/s).

medium.notes: Doubled master resources. 145 ops/msg × 300 = 43.5K ops/s, well within single-thread capacity with io_threads.

large.notes: Full E16s equivalent master. 145 ops/msg × 1000 = 145K ops/s. May need Redis Cluster sharding above this.
